description:
Context: You are acting as an artifact-manager who has to improve disk-usage performance inside an existing binary-repository tree located under /home/user/artifacts/. A few large “.bin” files are taking unnecessary space and must be compressed. The automated grader will examine the on-disk changes and a log file you create, so follow the instructions – especially the required log-file format – **exactly**. Starting state of the repository (already present on disk): • /home/user/artifacts/repo_alpha/file1.bin • /home/user/artifacts/repo_beta/module2/file2.bin • /home/user/artifacts/repo_beta/module3/file3.bin All three files are ≥ 1 MiB. Goal – you must produce these four observable results: 1. Every “*.bin” file that is **1 MiB (1 048 576 bytes) or larger** under /home/user/artifacts/ is replaced by a “*.bin.gz” file created with gzip compression. • The compressed file must live in the same directory and must be named “<original-name>.gz” • After successful compression, the original “*.bin” file must be deleted. 2. Create the directory /home/user/optimization_logs/ if it does not already exist. 3. Inside that directory create a CSV log called latest_optimization.log. 4. The log must use the **exact** format shown below so the grader can parse it: 
ORIGINAL_FILE_PATH,ORIGINAL_SIZE_BYTES,COMPRESSED_FILE_PATH,COMPRESSED_SIZE_BYTES,SAVED_BYTES
/home/user/artifacts/repo_alpha/file1.bin,1200000,/home/user/artifacts/repo_alpha/file1.bin.gz,????,????
/home/user/artifacts/repo_beta/module2/file2.bin,1200000,/home/user/artifacts/repo_beta/module2/file2.bin.gz,????,????
/home/user/artifacts/repo_beta/module3/file3.bin,1200000,/home/user/artifacts/repo_beta/module3/file3.bin.gz,????,????
TOTAL_SAVED_BYTES,XXXXX
• The header line must appear exactly once as the first line. • “ORIGINAL_FILE_PATH” and “COMPRESSED_FILE_PATH” must be absolute paths. • Lines for individual files must be sorted **lexicographically by ORIGINAL_FILE_PATH**. • Replace “????” with the real compressed-file sizes and the actual number of bytes saved for that row (SAVED_BYTES = ORIGINAL_SIZE_BYTES – COMPRESSED_SIZE_BYTES). • Replace “XXXXX” with the sum of the SAVED_BYTES column (no thousands separators). • Do **not** add extra spaces anywhere in the file. Constraints / hints: • Only files ≥ 1 MiB should be processed; if a new small “*.bin” appears, ignore it. • Use any gzip options you like, but the resulting file must end in “.gz”. • Make sure the log file ends with a single trailing newline (Unix line feed). • You do **not** need root privileges; everything is in your home directory. • A correct solution can be achieved with roughly 3–4 well-crafted shell commands. Verification: • The grader will confirm that each original “*.bin” file is gone, its “*.bin.gz” counterpart exists, and that every numeric value in /home/user/optimization_logs/latest_optimization.log is correct and internally consistent. • If any line is missing, out of order, or contains an incorrect field count, the task will be marked as failed. Perform the task now.
truth:
Files that exist before the agent starts:

    1. /home/user/artifacts/repo_alpha/file1.bin
       • Size: 1 200 000 bytes
       • Content: ASCII string “Lorem Ipsum\n” repeated 100 000 times

    2. /home/user/artifacts/repo_beta/module2/file2.bin
       • Size: 1 200 000 bytes
       • Content: identical to file1.bin (same repetition)

    3. /home/user/artifacts/repo_beta/module3/file3.bin
       • Size: 1 200 000 bytes
       • Content: identical to file1.bin (same repetition)

    Directory /home/user/optimization_logs/ does not exist initially.

    What the grader will verify after the agent finishes:

    a. The three original “*.bin” files no longer exist.  
    b. The following compressed files exist and are readable:
         • /home/user/artifacts/repo_alpha/file1.bin.gz
         • /home/user/artifacts/repo_beta/module2/file2.bin.gz
         • /home/user/artifacts/repo_beta/module3/file3.bin.gz
       (The grader will dynamically measure their size to validate the log.)
    c. /home/user/optimization_logs/latest_optimization.log exists and:
         • First line is exactly the five-column header listed in the task.  
         • Next three lines correspond to the three files, sorted alphabetically by ORIGINAL_FILE_PATH, with
           ORIGINAL_SIZE_BYTES = 1200000 and COMPRESSED_SIZE_BYTES matching the actual gzip file sizes;
           SAVED_BYTES = 1200000 − COMPRESSED_SIZE_BYTES.  
         • Final line starts with “TOTAL_SAVED_BYTES,” and the numeric field equals the sum of the three SAVED_BYTES
           values above.  
         • File ends with a single “\n”.
name: task_000000_87a7225a
